steps.title: Identify the noun requiring agreement
steps.content: The blank fills "of ______ nurses," where the word must modify the plural noun "nurses" and refer back to the thousands of nurses mentioned in the previous sentence.

steps.title: Apply the demonstrative agreement rule
steps.content:
A demonstrative adjective modifying a noun must agree with that noun in number. "Nurses" is plural, so the modifier must be plural too.

A) one — "one nurses" is ungrammatical; "one" requires a singular noun. Wrong.
B) that — "that" is a singular demonstrative; "that nurses" is ungrammatical. Wrong.
C) this — "this" is singular; "this nurses" is ungrammatical. Wrong.
D) these — "these" is the plural demonstrative and correctly modifies the plural noun "nurses." Correct.

D is correct.
